title: Self Storage — Buyer's Agent
features: Digital gate access installed 2 years ago
Month-to-month leases — flexible for rate increases
Established customer base — several tenants 5+ years

issues.item: 3 of 60 units are currently delinquent — 1 unit is 4 months behind, 2 units are 1 month behind
issues.pa_basis: Normal operational matter for self storage — 5% delinquency within industry norms; not a material physical defect
issues.seller_agent_action: Disclose current rent roll including delinquencies on income documentation.
issues.buyer_agent_action: Review full rent roll; factor delinquency rate into income underwriting; confirm lien process is current on 4-month unit.

issues.item: Two unit doors in Building B have alignment issues — stick when opening, operable with effort
issues.pa_basis: Minor physical wear — operational but in need of maintenance; not a structural or habitability issue
issues.seller_agent_action: Disclose if asked; repair credit appropriate.
issues.buyer_agent_action: Include in repair request list; estimate repair cost ~$400-600 for both.

issues.item: Drainage problem in the back lot — water pools after rain near Building C, has caused minor erosion along the eastern fence line
issues.pa_basis: Known water intrusion / drainage defect materially affecting property condition and potential liability (68 P.S. § 7304). Erosion may indicate ongoing structural risk to building foundation.
issues.seller_agent_action: Must disclose on Seller's Property Disclosure Statement. Cannot characterize as cosmetic.
issues.buyer_agent_action: Flag immediately. Request civil engineering assessment of drainage and erosion. Condition offer on satisfactory report. Adjust price or escrow for remediation cost.

client_persona.name: Dennis Park
client_persona.role: out-of-state investor from Atlanta — focused on remote management and operational metrics
client_persona.framing: Dennis is buying remotely and cares most about whether this property can run itself. He asks a lot of systems and management questions. He has a property manager in mind but wants to confirm the facility is solid before committing. He has not seen the drainage issue in person.
opening_lines: Hey, thanks for walking me through this on video. I'm in Atlanta so I'm relying on you to be my eyes. What's the biggest operational issue I should know about?
Appreciate the time. I've looked at the financials — the NOI looks good. Walk me through the physical first — what am I actually buying here?
behavioral_rules: Ask systems-focused questions: gate access, management software, maintenance history, staff setup
When the drainage issue comes up, ask: 'How long has that been an issue? Is it getting worse?'
If the agent tries to minimize the drainage: 'Look, I'm not there in person. If you're telling me there's water pooling near a building, I need a civil engineer on that before we go to contract.'
When delinquencies come up: 'Three units — what's the lien process? Is that current?'
Be professional and thorough — you have done this before and are not easily impressed or alarmed
Ask about the local market: 'Who else is operating storage nearby? What's their occupancy?'
curveballs: Ask if the seller would consider seller financing on part of the purchase
Mention you have a 45-day hard close timeline — is that possible given everything?
Ask whether the gate access system can be managed remotely — 'I'm not flying up here every month'
